---
title: "Programming Practice: Lab 3"
execute:
  enabled: true
  echo: false
  cache: true
format:
  html:
    code-fold: false
jupyter: python3
diagram:
  cache: true
  cache-dir: ./cache
  engine:
    tikz:
      execpath: lualatex
filters:
  - diagram
---

# SQL part 1

In this lab we will create a simple database backend for the BlockProcessor app.

This can be coded separately from [lab2](./lab2.qmd) (for now).

## Exercise 1
Please read and complete SQLite tutorial at <https://docs.python.org/3/library/sqlite3.html#sqlite3-tutorial>

## Exercise 2

Look at the below entity-relationship (ER) diagram:
```{mermaid}
erDiagram
BLOCKS {
 hexString id
 integer view
 string desc
 blob img
}

VOTES {
hexString block_id
integer voter_id
datetime timestamp
integer source_id
}

PERSONS {
integer id
string name
string addr
}

SOURCES {
integer id
string ip_addr
string country_code
}

BLOCKS ||..o{ VOTES : votes
VOTES }o..|| PERSONS : votes
SOURCES ||..o{ VOTES : originates
```

Please use Python's SQLite interface and write SQL queries for the following:

1. `CREATE TABLE` statements for the above 4 tables.
2. `INSERT INTO` statements to insert some test data into the tables.
